content: A Memory-Augmented Markov Decision Process can be redesigned to mirror the active learning mechanism of human biological memory, where the agent proactively queries its external memory buffer to resolve uncertainty, rather than passively retaining all past transitions. Just as human brains strengthen and prune memories based on how useful they are for solving new problems, this modified M-MDP would dynamically prioritize memory retrieval for high-uncertainty states that boost learning progress.
source_topics: memory as active learning human brain mechanisms
